title: WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: SILVERTON WIC OFFICE WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health program designed to improve health outcomes and influence lifetime nutrition and health behaviors for women, infants, and children. Nutrition education classes are provided as part of the program; other services include breastfeeding support, monthly benefits for supplemental nutritious foods, and referrals to community resources. 421 S Water Street Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Applicants must meet four criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is not required) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding woman, or an infant or child under age 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifySNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. ing child. -Have a household income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: 1st and 3rd Tuesdays of the month 9am-4pm (503) 588-5057 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 588-5057 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent/guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Marion County Services: WIC If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SILVERTON WIC OFFICE
